mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 24-month rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in >55% of rate increase months.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, it signals a deteriorating labor market that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate profit growth, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p>0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and consumer price inflation indicate robust aggregate demand outpacing supply constraints, which forces corporate earnings upward and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 20-day returns in >50% of triggered months, or the interaction term fails to improve model fit (F-test p>0.10) over single-series baselines.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which directly boosts short-term equity momentum and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal generates negative 5-day returns in >65% of observations during recovery phases, or the predictive power vanishes when GDP growth falls below 1.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls indicate expanding employment and wage growth, which increases household income and corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over any 24-month rolling window, or positive payroll revisions correlate with negative 20-day equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above a 0.4% monthly threshold triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally in >55% of months following CPI spikes above 0.4%, or the threshold effect disappears when real interest rates remain negative.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in housing starts and retail sales signals synchronized strength in durable goods demand and consumer confidence, which expands corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined housing and retail signal yields negative 20-day returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction term shows no incremental predictive power over individual series.

mechanism_narrative: Slowing real GDP growth below 1.5% annually signals weakening aggregate demand that reduces corporate earnings forecasts, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are positive in >60% of quarters where GDP growth drops below 1.5%, or the signal fails to predict returns when inflation remains below 2%.
